title: A −1 Ribosomal Frameshift in the Transcript That Encodes the Major Head Protein of Bacteriophage A2 Mediates Biosynthesis of a Second Essential Component of the Capsid
summary: The paper discusses Lactobacillus bacteriophage A2, which has two major capsid proteins (gp5A and gp5B) essential for phage viability, derived from a ribosomal frameshift mechanism. The phage was propagated on Lactobacillus casei ATCC 393 in MRS medium supplemented with CaCl2 and MgSO4 at 30°C. The study also mentions Escherichia phages T7 and T4, and Listeria phage PSA in the context of frameshift-derived proteins and capsid architecture.

Lactobacillus bacteriophage A2.growth_medium: liquid MRS medium (Oxoid) supplemented with 10 mM CaCl2 and 10 mM MgSO4 (MCM)

extra.frameshift_mechanism: requires a slippery sequence (C CCA AAA) and a stem-loop structure
extra.capsid_protein_ratio: 380 copies of gp5A and 110 copies of gp5B

extra.major_head_proteins: derived from a single gene through a +1 frameshift
